symptoms: Olive-green to black velvety spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves may drop early.
treatment: Use fungicides like Captan or Mancozeb. Prune to improve air circulation.
prevention: Rake and destroy fallen leaves. Plant resistant varieties.

symptoms: Purple spots on leaves (frogeye), reddish-brown rot on fruit, and cankers on limbs.
treatment: Prune out dead wood and cankers. Apply thiophanate-methyl fungicides.
prevention: Remove mummified fruit and debris from the orchard.

symptoms: Bright orange or yellow spots on the upper leaf surface. Tube-like structures on the underside.
treatment: Apply fungicides like Myclobutanil at the pink bud stage.
prevention: Remove nearby eastern red cedar trees if possible.

symptoms: White, powdery patches of fungus on leaves and fruit. Leaves may curl upward.
treatment: Apply sulfur or potassium bicarbonate fungicides early in the season.
prevention: Prune for better light and air penetration. Avoid excessive nitrogen.

symptoms: Cinnamon-brown pustules on both leaf surfaces that eventually turn black.
treatment: Apply foliar fungicides like Pyraclostrobin if infection is severe.
prevention: Use resistant corn hybrids and avoid overhead irrigation.

symptoms: Rectangular, gray to tan leaf lesions that run parallel to the leaf veins.
treatment: Apply fungicides like Azoxystrobin during the silking stage.
prevention: Rotate crops and use tilling to bury infected crop residue.

symptoms: Large, cigar-shaped, grayish-green to tan lesions on the leaves.
treatment: Apply fungicides like Propiconazole. Manage residue through tillage.
prevention: Plant resistant hybrids and ensure balanced soil nutrition.

symptoms: Small brown spots on leaves. Berries turn into hard, black, shriveled mummies.
treatment: Apply fungicides containing Mancozeb or Myclobutanil. Prune infected parts.
prevention: Keep vines off the ground and ensure full sun exposure.

symptoms: Interveinal yellowing of leaves (tiger-stripe). Dark spots on berry skin.
treatment: No effective chemical cure. Prune and protect large pruning wounds.
prevention: Use clean planting stock and avoid water stress in vines.

symptoms: Irregular reddish-brown spots on leaves. Severe cases lead to leaf drop.
treatment: Apply fungicides like Bordeaux mixture or Copper oxychloride.
prevention: Ensure proper vine spacing and destroy fallen leaves in winter.

symptoms: Small, water-soaked spots on leaves that turn brown and fall out (shot-hole).
treatment: Apply Copper-based sprays during dormancy or early spring.
prevention: Avoid highly susceptible varieties and use windbreaks.

symptoms: Small, dark, circular spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves turn yellow and drop.
treatment: Use Copper-based fungicides. Remove and destroy infected debris.
prevention: Use certified pathogen-free seeds and rotate crops annually.

symptoms: Small dark spots on older leaves with concentric rings (target-like).
treatment: Apply fungicides containing Chlorothalonil. Maintain high soil fertility.
prevention: Rotate crops with non-solanaceous plants and avoid overhead watering.

symptoms: Dark green water-soaked spots on leaves. White fungal growth on undersides.
treatment: Apply fungicides like Metalaxyl immediately. Destroy infected plants.
prevention: Use disease-free seed potatoes. Eliminate cull piles.

symptoms: Small purplish spots on the upper leaf surface that enlarge into irregular blotches.
treatment: Apply fungicides like Thiophanate-methyl if severe. Remove old leaves.
prevention: Plant in well-drained soil and avoid excessive nitrogen application.

symptoms: Small, dark, water-soaked spots on leaves and fruit. Leaves turn yellow.
treatment: Apply Copper-based fungicides. Disinfect tools frequently.
prevention: Avoid working in the field when plants are wet. Use clean seeds.

symptoms: Dark, concentric spots on lower leaves. May lead to stem lesions.
treatment: Apply fungicides containing Copper. Remove lower infected foliage.
prevention: Mulch to prevent soil-to-leaf contact and space plants properly.

symptoms: Large, dark water-soaked patches on leaves. Fruit turns hard and brown.
treatment: Immediate application of protective fungicides. Burn infected plants.
prevention: Use resistant varieties and maintain low humidity in greenhouses.

symptoms: Pale greenish-yellow spots on upper leaf surfaces. Olive-green mold on undersides.
treatment: Apply fungicides like Difenoconazole. Improve greenhouse ventilation.
prevention: Reduce humidity below 85% and use drip irrigation instead of spray.

symptoms: Small, circular spots with dark margins and gray/tan centers on lower leaves.
treatment: Apply Copper or Chlorothalonil fungicides. Remove infected leaves.
prevention: Destroy all plant debris at the end of the season. Use stakes.

symptoms: Severe stunting. Leaves curl upward and turn bright yellow between veins.
treatment: No chemical cure for the virus. Control the whitefly population.
prevention: Use insect nets and yellow sticky traps to catch whiteflies.
